prevalence.source: Occupational health and musculoskeletal epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; higher in manual labor and repetitive work

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant pain and work limitation

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical tendon pain with activity and focal tenderness; ultrasound or MRI if diagnosis uncertain

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions of cases across musculoskeletal clinics
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with physiotherapy and ergonomic changes

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; imaging access variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Activity modification, NSAIDs, physiotherapy, ergonomic interventions; injections for selected cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to physiotherapy and workplace ergonomics
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective conservative management options and affordable NSAIDs
